REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANTS BALDINI REAL ESTATE, INC. AND BRUNO BALDINI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND NEGLIGENCE ARE BASED ON SEVERAL DISTINCT WRONGDOINGS. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED AS THE CLAIMS OF "CAUSING UNPERMITTED WORK" AND "SUPPLYING UNLICENSED CONTRACTORS." CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION IS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED (501/REQ)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANTS BALDINI REAL ESTATE, INC. AND BRUNO BALDINI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT

Appearances: Nicholas Peter Burke of Musacchio & Montari, PC (925.465.3950) for cross-defendants and moving parties; Arthur Meirson of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad, Harris & McSparran LLP (415.673.5600) for cross-complainant and moving responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty and negligence are based on several distinct wrongdoings. These causes of action are not barred by the Statute of Limitations and are sufficiently alleged as the claims of "causing unpermitted work" and "supplying unlicensed contractors." Cause of action for conversion is sufficiently alleged. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANTS BALDINI REAL ESTATE, INC. AND BRUNO BALDINI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT

Appearances: Nicholas Peter Burke of Musacchio & Montari, PC (925.465.3950) for cross-defendants and moving parties; Arthur Meirson of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad, Harris & McSparran LLP (415.673.5600) for cross-complainant and moving responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Request for punitive damages is sufficiently alleged. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey H. Belote (415-984-8508) for Plaintiff/Moving party MORIA FONG, LINCOLN FONG, ALEXANDER LEW, TINGLEY LEW.
Arthur Meirson and A. Jeanne Grove (415-673-5600) for Defendant/opposing party Kenneth Lew.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: bond, property manager, referee, entitlement to payment, unpermitted work, duty, attempted eviction, expenses, records and accounting. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFs MORIA FONG, LINCOLN FONG, ALEXANDER LEW, TINGLEY LEW's Motion For Preliminary Injunction is taken under submission. Both sides submits proposed orders. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
